findings.description: Nested <main> elements in the HTML. The document has a <main> wrapper and then another <main> inside it, which is invalid HTML structure but does not affect content fidelity.
findings.evidence: The outer <main> from the template wraps an inner <main> that contains the ordered list content.

findings.description: The URL in the case studies section is present as plain text but not rendered as a clickable hyperlink in the HTML, matching the PDF which also presents it as plain text. No fabricated links.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'https://www.deq.nc.gov/about/divisions/wastemanagement/superfund-section/dry-cleaning-solvent-cleanup-act-program/dscastakeholder-work-group-information' — HTML presents the same URL as plain text with slightly different hyphenation: 'https://www.deq.nc.gov/about/divisions/waste-management/superfund-section/dry-cleaning-solvent-cleanup-act-program/dsca-stakeholder-work-group-information'

findings.description: Minor difference in URL formatting: the PDF shows the URL with line breaks mid-word (e.g., 'wastemanagement' and 'dscastakeholder'), while the HTML normalizes these with hyphens ('waste-management' and 'dsca-stakeholder'). This likely reflects the actual intended URL and is arguably more correct.
